# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

# Define temporary file path with a predictable name
temp_file="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"

# Function to create and write data to the temporary file
create_temp_file() {
    echo "Creating and writing to temp file $temp_file"
    echo "Sample data line 1" > "$temp_file"
    echo "Sample data line 2" >> "$temp_file"
}

# Function to process the data in the temporary file
process_data() {
    echo "Processing data from $temp_file"
    sort "$temp_file" > "$temp_file.tmp"
    mv "$temp_file.tmp" "$temp_file"
}

# Function to read the processed data from the temporary file
read_data() {
    echo "Reading processed data from $temp_file"
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        echo "$line"
    done < "$temp_file"
}

# Main script execution
create_temp_file
process_data
read_data

# Clean up: remove the temporary file
rm -f "$temp_file"

echo "Script execution completed."
